title: PRD: Minimal Agent Framework (MAF)
description: Epic tracking delivery of the Minimal Agent Framework PRD, MVP runtime, tooling, and replayability.

title: Keys & Access
description: Collect and verify prerequisites before implementation: provider keys, strict HTTP allowlist, required services, local CLIs, preview/E2E access strategy, and Playwright system dependencies for browser-based verification.
acceptance_criteria:
Document required secrets and optional aliases in .env.example
Document required services/accounts and ownership
Document required CLIs and minimum versions
Document Playwright/Linux system dependencies when E2E/browser checks are used
Define E2E/preview protection bypass strategy (custom domain, bypass token/header, or approved disablement)
close_reason: Prerequisites documented in PRD.md and .env.example, including preview/E2E bypass plan and Playwright system dependencies.

title: MVP validation harness (golden traces)
description: Create small validation harness that runs deterministic scenarios and compares against expected trace snapshots.
acceptance_criteria:
At least three deterministic scenarios validated (final-only, tool success, tool timeout)
Golden trace fixtures are versioned and easy to refresh
Harness exits non-zero on structural trace regression
README documents how to run local validation

title: Runtime kernel loop (read/think/decide/act/write)
description: Implement AgentRuntime.run with max-step and wall-clock budget guards, halt reasons, and deterministic step transitions.
acceptance_criteria:
Runtime executes step loop until final or halt
Supports max steps and max run seconds
Records halt reason for budget/time/cancel paths
Unit tests cover normal completion and halt edges
close_reason: Implemented step-based runtime loop with max-step and max-runtime guards, halt reasons, and unit tests for completion/max-step/max-runtime paths.

title: LLM adapter contract + mock provider
description: Define provider-agnostic adapter interface and add a deterministic mock adapter to unblock trace/replay tests.
acceptance_criteria:
Adapter interface supports model call request/response metadata
At least one real provider config path exists
Mock adapter can deterministically emit final and tool_call actions
Tests verify runtime works against adapter abstraction

title: Typed tool registry + schema validation
description: Create tool registration primitives with input/output schemas, timeout config, and runtime validation before execution.
acceptance_criteria:
Tools register with name/description/input schema/output schema/timeout
Runtime rejects invalid tool-call payloads before execution
Validation failures are trace-recorded as structured errors
Tests cover valid and invalid tool-call paths

title: Implement MVP power tools (shell/fs/http/kv)
description: Implement minimal tool set with strict defaults: local shell.exec, fs read/write/list, http.fetch allowlist + timeout, kv get/set file-backed store.
acceptance_criteria:
shell.exec supports timeout and captures stdout/stderr/exit code
fs operations are limited to configured root path
http.fetch enforces allowlist and timeout
kv.get/kv.set persists values on disk and is test-covered

title: Structured trace events + JSONL persistence
description: Emit required lifecycle events and persist run trace/state snapshots in JSONL for local inspection.
acceptance_criteria:
Events include run_started, model_called, tool_called, tool_result, run_finished, error
Trace writer persists JSONL per run_id with stable schema
Final state persisted and linked from run metadata
Trace output can be consumed by a debug command

title: Replay mode with recorded tool results
description: Add replay mode that reuses prior tool results to reproduce control flow without re-executing tools.
acceptance_criteria:
Replay command can run from a prior run_id
Tool execution is bypassed and replaced with recorded outputs
Replay trace clearly marks replayed tool results
Tests validate deterministic replay on fixed adapter outputs

title: CLI surface (run, trace, replay)
description: Expose user-facing CLI commands for executing runs, printing trace views, and replaying historic runs.
acceptance_criteria:
maf run accepts input and optional state/config params
maf trace prints step-oriented debug view for a run_id
maf replay executes from stored trace/state artifacts
CLI help includes examples and expected flags

title: Event streaming + cancellation UX
description: Add high-level event streaming and ctrl+c cancellation handling without corrupting trace/state outputs.
acceptance_criteria:
Event stream mode emits step and tool events in order
Cancellation produces explicit halted event and persisted partial trace
No orphaned temp files/processes after cancellation
Manual test script documents expected interrupt behavior
